question: Define a C++ class named `Book` with private member variables for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Implement public methods to `setTitle`, `setAuthor`, `setISBN` and `displayBookInfo` which prints all details.

test_cases.expected:
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
ISBN: 978-0345391803

Title: 1984
Author: George Orwell
ISBN: 978-0451524935

Title: 
Author: Unknown
ISBN: INVALID-ISBN

errors: Lack of input validation in setter methods allows for invalid or problematic data (e.g., empty title, malformed ISBN) to be stored in the Book object, reducing the robustness of the data model.
